title: salix exigua var. exterior
intro: Salix exigua var. exterior, also known as the desert cottonwood or coyote willow, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Native American medicine, to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Salix exigua var. exterior include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Salix exigua var. exterior can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
